hero.title: Morbid Metal
hero.description: Morbid Metal is a fast-paced dark sci-fi action roguelite built around real-time shapeshifting, stylish combo chains, boss fights, and replayable progression.

gameFeature.description: Experience dark sci-fi combat with real-time shapeshifting, stylish combo chains, and roguelite progression in Morbid Metal's Early Access build.

morbidMetalBeginnerGuide.title: Morbid Metal Beginner Guide
morbidMetalBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Morbid Metal rewards controlled swapping, clean target priority, and smart hub spending far more than button mashing. This guide gives new players a practical route through the first biomes.
morbidMetalBeginnerGuide.intro: The early game becomes much easier once you stop treating Morbid Metal like a single-character action game. Your best results come from rotating roles, buying a few key permanent upgrades early, and protecting your health economy so every run reaches the boss with real momentum.

steps.title: Play the whole squad, not just Flux
steps.description: The combat system is built around real-time switching. Flux starts fights quickly, Vekta creates breathing room and ranged pressure, and Ekku cashes out launches and crowd control with heavy damage.
takeaways: Do not stay on one character out of habit
Switch to keep cooldowns cycling instead of waiting them out
Treat every encounter like a rotation instead of a solo combo string

steps.title: Clear flying and ranged threats first
steps.description: Fast aerial and off-screen pressure is one of the easiest ways to lose health in the first biome. Flux is especially effective at reaching airborne enemies quickly, and later Vekta can safely pick them off from range.
takeaways: Delete drones and ranged harassers before fighting heavies
Use Flux mobility to close distance immediately
Once Vekta joins, use projectiles to stabilize chaotic rooms

steps.title: Build around role-matched Routines
steps.description: Routine rewards are strongest when they support what each character already does well. Leak-focused Routines stand out early because the status keeps dealing damage while you dodge, reposition, and set up your next swap.
takeaways: Prioritize Leak and Leak-spread effects when they appear
Avoid picking buffs that do not fit your actual rotation
Think in terms of opener, control, and finisher roles

steps.title: Buy the four early hub upgrades immediately
steps.description: The first permanent upgrades that smooth out the game are Perfect Dodge Counter, Ka-ching!, Iron Resilience, and Run Bargain Unlock. Together they improve survivability, economy, and access to stronger risk-reward runs.
takeaways: Perfect Dodge Counter: free and instantly useful
Ka-ching!: boosts Void Matter income by 10 percent per level
Iron Resilience: adds 10 max health
Run Bargain Unlock: opens Devil's Bargains for stronger scaling

steps.title: Protect your healing economy mid-run
steps.description: Healing is scarce and should be planned around. The main healing sources are Emporium healing, Healing Nodes, Vitality Fabricators, and specific Routine-based recovery effects.
takeaways: Keep some credits available when you are already damaged
Emporium healing is the most reliable baseline heal
Run Fabricator Unlock increases Vitality Fabricator frequency over time
Defensive Routines are worth mixing in, but not at the cost of all damage

steps.title: Explore side paths before the biome boss
steps.description: Runs scale better when you check hidden routes, gather extra resources, and enter the boss fight with a real Corpora advantage. Strong Corpora picks such as Fountain of Life or Stun Matrix can turn an average run into a winning one.
takeaways: Do not sprint straight to objective markers every time
Memorize static room features and hidden Repository spots
Aim to reach the boss with at least one meaningful Corpora power spike

quickTips: Swap characters to cycle cooldowns rather than waiting them out on one form.
Perfect Dodge Counter is free and should be bought before anything else at the hub.
Leak status deals damage over time while you reposition, making it one of the best early Routine effects.
Check hidden side rooms before boss encounters to maximize Corpora and credit advantages.

morbidMetalBestUpgrades.title: Morbid Metal Best Upgrades
morbidMetalBestUpgrades.subtitle: The fastest way to make Morbid Metal easier is to improve your dodge value, health, income, and skill uptime before chasing late-run luxury nodes.
morbidMetalBestUpgrades.intro: Permanent upgrades define how quickly your account stops feeling fragile. Health, economy, cooldown, and damage all pay off immediately, while deeper Corpora and build-slot nodes become much stronger once your basic run stability is already in place.

items.why_it_matters: Makes early bosses and surprise hits survivable
Core economy node that speeds up every future unlock
Skills are the backbone of your best swap combos
Improves DPS and shortens melee exposure windows
Corpora is one of the best scaling systems in the game
Pushes scaling further once base stability is solved
High-risk, high-reward modifiers for aggressive builds
Enables complex late-game loadouts and specialization

morbidMetalBestSkillsAndCombos.title: Morbid Metal Best Skills and Combos
morbidMetalBestSkillsAndCombos.subtitle: Morbid Metal combat opens up once you stop thinking in isolated moves and start thinking in role-based chains. These are the most practical early picks and combo routes.
morbidMetalBestSkillsAndCombos.intro: The strongest early setups combine Flux mobility, Ekku launch or crowd control, and Vekta spacing. Flux and Ekku already have clear recommended Protocol winners, while Vekta's job is to keep pressure safe, control the screen, and make swaps cleaner.

cards.why_it_works: Blink Strike offers the best flexibility for longer fights, movement, and anti-air. Nano Strike phases Flux out while dealing damage, making it both evasive and aggressive.

key_data: Seismic Rift: up to 1100 damage, special cost 3
cards.why_it_works: Upslam is preferred over Ground Shatter because it launches targets and opens airborne routes. Sonic Slash is more flexible in mixed-character runs.

key_data: Swap to Flux before enemies fall
Finish with Ekku ground slam for burst
cards.why_it_works: This is one of the cleanest early combo routes because it uses Ekku's launch utility and Flux's faster aerial follow-up. It teaches the core rhythm: opener, extension, finisher.

best_picks: Open with projectiles at safe range
Reposition, then hand off to Flux or Ekku
key_data: Uses projectiles, cybernetic shurikens, and force-pull style control
Strong for stabilizing rooms and picking off flying enemies
cards.why_it_works: Vekta is the safest way to reset pace when the camera gets messy or melee pressure stacks up. She helps group targets, buy space, and feed cleaner finishers to Flux and Ekku.

cards.card_title: Morbid Metal Boss and Elite Adaptation
best_picks: Back Stab for burst on tougher enemies
Swap to Ekku for shields and heavies
key_data: Ekku is stronger into shielded and heavy targets
cards.why_it_works: Blink Strike is the better all-purpose pick, but Back Stab has stronger burst against bruisers and bosses, especially when paired with crit-supporting Routines.

morbidMetalCharactersGuide.title: Morbid Metal Characters Guide
morbidMetalCharactersGuide.subtitle: Morbid Metal's three characters are built to solve different combat problems. Understanding when each one joins your run and what role they fill makes the swap system click much faster.
morbidMetalCharactersGuide.intro: The cast is small in Early Access, but the roles are sharply defined. Flux is your fast opener, Ekku is your heavy control finisher, and Vekta is your ranged stabilizer. Good runs come from switching to the right answer instead of trying to brute-force every fight with one favorite form.

characters.role: High single-target damage and fast opener
strengths: Excellent for starting combos and chasing stragglers
characters.team_flow: Open fights with Flux, create the first launch or stagger, then swap before melee pressure surrounds you.

characters.role: Slow heavy hitter with crowd control and shield pressure
strengths: Great at taking out heavies and knocking down shields
characters.team_flow: Swap into Ekku after Flux or Vekta has set the room up, then cash out grouped enemies or shielded targets with heavy burst.

strengths: Uses projectiles, cybernetic shurikens, and force-pull style control
Very effective at calming chaotic screens and cleaning up flying threats
characters.team_flow: Bring Vekta in when spacing matters, when ranged enemies are forcing bad angles, or when you need a safer handoff into the next melee finisher.

characters.unlock_timing: Available once the full team is assembled
strengths: Vekta buys space or groups targets
Ekku finishes launched, grouped, or shielded enemies
characters.team_flow: A reliable early rhythm is Flux to engage, Vekta to stabilize, then Ekku to finish. Against fliers, start with Flux or Vekta first; against heavies and shields, end on Ekku.

morbidMetalBossGuide.title: Morbid Metal Boss Guide
morbidMetalBossGuide.subtitle: Biome Boss Strategies for Saru and Prophet
morbidMetalBossGuide.intro: Morbid Metal currently ends each biome with a boss that checks whether your run has enough control, survivability, and swap discipline. Saru is the first true mechanics test, while Prophet is the tougher second-biome fight that punishes greedy combo routes. A clean plan matters more than random aggression, especially when your healing and cooldowns are limited.

bosses.summary: Saru is the Biome 1 boss and the first full mechanics check of Early Access. The fight mixes leap pressure, ground impact attacks, orb patterns, and a later shield-break window.

sections.label: Opening pattern
sections.content: Expect fast jumps into heavy slam attacks. Stay just outside the impact zone, dodge late, and only punish after the landing animation finishes.

sections.label: Orb pressure
sections.content: When the arena starts filling with orb patterns, keep moving with the pattern instead of cutting straight through it. A smooth circling route keeps the screen readable and gives you time to hold your swap tools.

sections.label: Best form usage
sections.content: Ekku is the safest pick for control and shield-break pressure, while Flux is the clean burst finisher once Saru is open. Use swaps to turn a short opening into a real damage window.

sections.label: How to close the fight
sections.content: Treat post-slam recovery and shield-break moments as your main damage windows. Short, repeatable punish loops are safer than forcing one long combo.

bosses.summary: Prophet is the Biome 2 boss and the second major wall of the current build. This fight rewards calmer spacing, tighter reactions, and smarter form rotation more than reckless offense.

sections.label: Core plan
sections.content: Go in with a stable build and keep one movement option ready at all times. Prophet punishes overextension, so the safest route is to play around clear attack endings instead of swinging through every opening.

sections.label: Damage pattern
sections.content: Use Vekta to keep pressure from safer range, rotate into Flux for fast burst, and bring Ekku in when you need a heavier punish or more control. The fight feels cleaner when every swap has a purpose.

sections.label: Arena discipline
sections.content: Do not spend every dodge as soon as pressure starts. Hold your evade for the real threat, reset your spacing after each punish, and avoid turning a good opening into a bad trade.

sections.label: What actually wins
sections.content: Status pressure, controlled swaps, and consistent punish windows win this fight more reliably than raw face-tanking. If the run is shaky, play for stability first and style rank second.

morbidMetalAchievementGuide.title: Morbid Metal Achievement Guide
morbidMetalAchievementGuide.subtitle: All 13 Early Access Achievements
morbidMetalAchievementGuide.intro: This table is built for players chasing a clean 100% route without bouncing between menus. The fastest approach is to grab the natural progression achievements on the way to Biome 2, then clean up the skill checks and token grind afterward.

items.unlock: Buy your first upgrade in the Neural Nexus
items.best_route: Spend your first return-trip currency in the hub as soon as the system opens.
Take the first Void Rift you naturally find during a run.
Comes naturally through early Biome 1 progression.
Enter a Trial when your health and cooldowns are in a good place.
Check side paths and interactables instead of only sprinting the main lane.

items.unlock: Reach your first SSS stylerank in a combat
items.best_route: Use clean swaps, avoid getting clipped, and build the rank in a safer multi-enemy room.
Defeat Saru and finish Sublime Garden.
Push your progression into the second biome and roster expansion will follow.
Take one bargain once the system appears, even if it is not part of your ideal build.
Defeat Prophet and finish Steel Sanctuary.
Slot your first Corpora as soon as the system becomes available.
This is the long-term cleanup achievement, so keep playing full runs after the natural unlocks are done.

morbidMetalCombatGuide.title: Morbid Metal Combat Guide
morbidMetalCombatGuide.subtitle: How the Switching Combat System Actually Works
morbidMetalCombatGuide.intro: The game feels best once you stop treating the roster as separate characters and start treating them as one shared combo engine. Flux starts pressure, Vekta extends control from range, and Ekku stabilizes fights with stronger crowd control and heavier punish tools. The more cleanly you rotate between them, the more the system opens up.

items.summary: The core mechanic is instant swapping mid-combat.
details: You can switch characters during fights instead of committing to one form.
Swaps let you extend combos, reposition, and keep pressure going after a short opening.
The combat loop is designed around chaining multiple forms together rather than playing one fighter in isolation.

items.summary: Leak is one of the strongest early combat mechanics.
details: Leak is a stackable damage-over-time effect.
It deals damage every second, stacks for higher damage, and can spread between enemies.
Leak builds become powerful because they turn normal clears into chain-reaction clears.

items.summary: Each form fills a different combat job in the Early Access build.
details: Flux is the precision damage form and is especially good at applying Leak quickly.
Vekta is the tactical ranged form that helps control fights and spread pressure more safely.
Ekku is the crowd-control form and is especially valuable when you need more stable boss punish windows.

items.summary: Morbid Metal plays closer to a stylish action game than a basic roguelite brawler.
details: Ability-driven combo chains matter more than simple attack spam.
Precision dodging and timing are part of the offense, not just defense.
Good combat comes from flow: apply status, swap cleanly, punish, then reset before the next string.

items.summary: The cleanest runs usually focus on a few linked ideas, not random upgrades.
details: Aiming for two to three strong synergies is more reliable than scattering upgrades everywhere.
Flux's Blink Strike is flexible because it adds both mobility and damage pressure.
Ekku's control tools are useful for setting up follow-up damage from the rest of the team.

items.summary: Input speed and route discipline matter more than button mashing.
details: Mouse and keyboard can make rapid swap execution easier for some players.
Fast, clean inputs help maintain combo flow and reduce dropped punish windows.
The best damage usually comes from short repeatable routes, not one risky overextended string.

morbidMetalHowToHeal.title: Morbid Metal How to Heal
morbidMetalHowToHeal.subtitle: Every Reliable Way to Recover or Preserve Health
morbidMetalHowToHeal.intro: A low-health run is not automatically dead, but you need to know what counts as real healing and what only reduces incoming damage. The core idea is simple: save credits, respect side paths, and treat shop access as a lifeline instead of a bonus stop.

items.label: Buy a slight heal in the Emporium
items.detail: The most direct recovery source is the Emporium, the mid-run shop reached through a Void Rift. It can sell a slight heal and is the most dependable way to patch up a damaged run.

items.label: Check side paths for healing nodes
items.detail: Healing nodes are real but not common. If your health is shaky, it is worth slowing down and checking side routes instead of speed-running straight to the next fight.

items.label: Use Vitality Fabricators when they appear
items.detail: Vitality Fabricators are rarer than shop healing, but they become a major sustain source once you start seeing them more often. They are especially strong because you can buy multiple heals if you have enough credits.

items.label: Unlock more Vitality Fabricator access
items.detail: The Run Fabricator Unlock in the Void Nexus increases the chance that Vitality Fabricators appear. This turns a lucky sustain mechanic into a much more dependable part of your routing.

items.label: Use shop-entry sustain like Corpora Herbal Tea
items.detail: Corpora Herbal Tea restores a portion of your max life when entering the Shop. It is not a universal answer, but it makes every Emporium visit more valuable.

items.label: Take barrier or shield routines on unstable runs
items.detail: Some Routines give a barrier at the start of combat or add shields when using a special skill. These do not replace true healing, but they reduce how often you need it.

items.label: Always keep emergency credits
items.detail: Do not spend every credit the moment you get it. Leaving some currency unspent gives you a better chance to turn an Emporium or Vitality Fabricator appearance into an actual reset point.

morbidMetalBuildsGuide.title: Morbid Metal Builds Guide
morbidMetalBuildsGuide.subtitle: These Morbid Metal builds focus on the clearest Early Access archetypes supported by the current skill pool, character-switch routes, and permanent upgrade priorities.
morbidMetalBuildsGuide.intro: Morbid Metal does not lock players into one rigid meta yet, but the current Early Access build already shows several strong patterns. Flux is excellent for mobility and burst, Ekku anchors safer crowd control and launcher routes, and switching between characters is one of the game's biggest damage multipliers.

builds.name: Flux Blink Assassin
builds.description: Blink Strike gives Flux the most flexible opener and movement tool, while Nano Strike adds phase-style damage and helps you stay aggressive without standing still. This is the cleanest high-speed build in Early Access and the easiest one to keep relevant across a full run.

builds.name: Ekku Air Launcher
builds.playstyle: Crowd control into airborne combo routes
builds.description: Upslam is the clearest Ekku starter for launch routes, Echo Strike gives efficient area coverage, and Sonic Slash is easier to fit into mixed-character runs. A strong build for players who want structure and safety without giving up combo flow.

builds.name: Ekku Main Bruiser
builds.playstyle: Heavy damage and shield-breaking with Ekku on field longer
builds.description: Seismic Rift has the bigger damage profile, but it shines most when Ekku is your main active fighter rather than a quick swap tool. This setup works best when you want a slower, sturdier run that converts stun and pressure into boss damage.

builds.name: Swap Combo Loop
builds.playstyle: Character-switch chaining instead of single-character commitment
core_skills: Optional Vekta follow-up for wider coverage
best_for: Players who want the full shapeshift identity
builds.description: Morbid Metal's core hook is free character switching, and the strongest expressive playstyle is the one that launches with Ekku and cashes out with Flux. Less about one protocol being broken and more about turning swaps into constant pressure and combo extensions.

morbidMetalReleaseDateRoadmap.title: Morbid Metal Release Date and Roadmap
morbidMetalReleaseDateRoadmap.subtitle: Morbid Metal launched into Steam Early Access on April 8, 2026. The official launch materials already define the starting scope, the flexible Year One update approach, and the content categories planned for expansion.
morbidMetalReleaseDateRoadmap.intro: This is the practical version of a Morbid Metal roadmap. The official materials confirm when Early Access began, what the launch build contains, how the team plans to expand it, and where community feedback feeds into future updates.

entries.details: Ubisoft confirmed that Morbid Metal would enter Steam Early Access on April 8, 2026 and framed the release as the start of player-guided development.

entries.event: Morbid Metal Launches on Steam Early Access
entries.details: The Steam store lists April 8, 2026 as both the release date and the Early Access release date. The launch price is $17.99, with an introductory discount bringing it to $13.49 during the launch offer window.

entries.event: Starting Scope — Over 10 Hours of Content
entries.details: Official launch materials describe over 10 hours of content with three playable characters, two biomes, two bosses, a Void tutorial, a Void Hub, a skill tree, and run progression systems such as Routines, Devil's Bargains, Eden Blessings, Nano Chips, The Emporium, and Repositories.

entries.event: Year One Roadmap — Broader Content Growth
entries.details: The team already has a Year One roadmap and plans to expand with a new playable character, additional environments, and new bosses, while keeping the timing flexible around community feedback.

Updates and discussion run through the Steam Community Hub and the official Discord. Players who buy into Early Access are expected to receive future updates, including the final version, at no additional charge.

morbidMetalSystemRequirements.title: Morbid Metal System Requirements
morbidMetalSystemRequirements.subtitle: Steam lists one official minimum spec and one recommended spec for the current Early Access build. The game also supports Xbox and PlayStation controllers, and is Steam Deck compatible.
morbidMetalSystemRequirements.intro: Both spec tiers use Windows 10 or 11, DirectX 11, and 30 GB of storage. The recommended tier moves up to 16 GB RAM and a mid-range GPU target, making this a clean comparison for both desktop and mobile players.

specs.gpu: Nvidia GTX 1650 4GB / AMD RX 5500 4GB / Intel Arc A380 6GB
Nvidia RTX 2060 6GB / AMD RX 5700 8GB / Intel Arc A750 8GB

notes: Xbox and PlayStation controller support is included
Steam Deck compatible — listed on the official Steam store page
DirectX 11 is required for both spec tiers
30 GB of storage is needed for both minimum and recommended setups

morbidMetalReviewImpressions.title: Morbid Metal Review and Impressions
morbidMetalReviewImpressions.subtitle: The early reception for Morbid Metal is built around recurring themes: stylish combat, strong character-switching, impressive presentation, and a launch build that already feels polished for Early Access.
morbidMetalReviewImpressions.intro: The cards below summarize the practical consensus from launch-window coverage and current user sentiment — focusing on combat feel, content depth, and what still feels like work in progress.

highlights: Fast-paced combat feels satisfying and responsive
Distinct playable characters with genuine identity
Strong audiovisual polish for an Early Access launch — 79% positive from 362 reviews
watchouts: Still an unfinished Early Access game with smaller content scope
Content depth will grow as the roadmap delivers new areas and characters

highlights: Combat feels satisfying and technically nuanced
Character switching gives each encounter tactical variety
World design and exploration are stronger than expected
watchouts: Progression is still a slow-burn unlock path
Final judgment depends on how future updates land

highlights: Free character switching is the real hook — base kits have enough depth before RNG bonuses
Mobility and traversal feel fast and satisfying
Combat foundation is already real — most complaints look like Early Access tuning targets
watchouts: A mini map would improve room-to-room readability
Cooldowns on specials can occasionally interrupt combat flow

highlights: Permanent upgrades keep failed runs productive
Current build already feels polished — recommended for action-roguelike fans
watchouts: Difficulty still expects good dodge timing from the player
Larger roster and more areas will require waiting for content updates

morbidMetalDemoGuide.title: Morbid Metal Demo Guide
morbidMetalDemoGuide.subtitle: The Morbid Metal demo gave players an early look at the combat system before Early Access. Here is what the demo included and how it compares to the current build.
morbidMetalDemoGuide.intro: The Morbid Metal demo was available on Steam before the Early Access launch, giving players a taste of the shapeshifting combat and first biome. The demo and the current EA build differ in scope and content depth.

faqs.question: Is the Morbid Metal demo still available?
faqs.answer: The demo (Steam App ID 3762220) was available as a separate listing before Early Access launched. Once Early Access begins, demo availability depends on the developer's decision to keep it live alongside the main game.

faqs.question: What did the Morbid Metal demo include?
faqs.answer: The demo included a portion of Biome 1 (Sublime Garden) and the core shapeshifting combat system. Flux was playable in the demo, giving players a feel for the fast-paced combat before committing to purchase.

faqs.question: How does the demo compare to Early Access?
faqs.answer: The full Early Access build adds Ekku and Vekta as playable characters, additional biomes, the full hub upgrade system (Void Matter progression), the Corpora boon system, and Devil's Bargain modifiers. The demo represents a small fraction of the current Early Access content.

faqs.question: Should I play the demo before buying?
faqs.answer: If the demo is still available, it is a useful way to confirm that the combat feel clicks with you before purchasing. The game's entire identity is built around the shapeshifting system, and the demo communicates it clearly.

morbidMetalFluxGuide.title: Morbid Metal Flux Guide
morbidMetalFluxGuide.subtitle: Flux is the fastest character in Morbid Metal and your default opener for most encounters. Mastering Flux's mobility and skill timing makes the entire swap system smoother.
morbidMetalFluxGuide.intro: Flux is the character you will spend the most time with early in every run. Her speed, anti-air capabilities, and flexible skill options make her the ideal opener for most encounter types.

cards.name: Role: Fast Opener
cards.description: Flux starts fights quickly and pressures enemies before they can group up. Her job is to initiate, create the first stagger or launch, and set up Ekku or Vekta for follow-up damage.

cards.name: Best Skills
cards.description: Blink Strike (140 damage, 8s cooldown) is the all-purpose pick for mobility and anti-air. Nano Strike phases Flux out while dealing damage, adding an evasive dimension. Power Blade offers the strongest burst for those committed to Flux-heavy loadouts.

cards.name: Anti-Air Specialist
cards.description: Flux is the best character for targeting airborne enemies in the first biome. Her Blink Strike closes vertical distance instantly, making her the default answer when drones or ranged fliers appear.

cards.name: Combo Starter
cards.description: Most high-value combo routes begin with Flux because she can engage the fastest and apply the first stagger. Once targets are in the air or stunned, swap to Ekku for the launch follow-up or Vekta for ranged clean-up.

morbidMetalEkkuGuide.title: Morbid Metal Ekku Guide
morbidMetalEkkuGuide.subtitle: Ekku is your crowd control and burst damage specialist. Knowing when to rotate into Ekku is the key to dealing with shielded enemies, heavies, and boss punish windows.
morbidMetalEkkuGuide.intro: Ekku unlocks in Biome 1 (Sublime Garden) and immediately becomes the team's finisher. Her slower pace is offset by multi-hit attacks, shield breaking, and the strongest slam damage in the roster.

cards.name: Role: Finisher and Shield Breaker
cards.description: Ekku's job is to cash out the setups created by Flux and Vekta. She excels at grouped enemies, shielded heavies, and punish windows after boss attacks.

cards.name: Best Skills
cards.description: Upslam (220 damage, 14s cooldown) is preferred over Ground Shatter because it launches targets and opens airborne routes. Seismic Rift (up to 1100 damage) is the best boss damage option when paired with a reliable punish window.

cards.name: Shield and Heavy Specialist
cards.description: When enemies have shields or high poise, swap to Ekku. Her attack weight and multi-hit patterns break defensive stances faster than Flux or Vekta. Heavies that survive the first room are almost always Ekku targets.

cards.name: Slam Combo Finisher
cards.description: The core use of Ekku in a swap combo is to enter after Flux has launched a target and finish with a slam. This two-step sequence is the most reliable high-damage window in the early game and scales well with Skill Issue upgrades.

morbidMetalVektaGuide.title: Morbid Metal Vekta Guide
morbidMetalVektaGuide.subtitle: Vekta is the team's ranged specialist and screen stabilizer. Learning when to swap into Vekta prevents most of the chip damage that comes from chaotic room states.
morbidMetalVektaGuide.intro: Vekta unlocks in Biome 2 and completes the team's full rotation. Her ranged projectiles, cybernetic shurikens, and force-pull style control add a completely different pace to encounters.

cards.name: Role: Ranged Stabilizer
cards.description: Vekta's job is to reset the pace when rooms get chaotic. Swap into Vekta when enemies are spreading out, when ranged threats are dominating the screen, or when you need to group targets for Ekku's area attacks.

cards.name: Best Use Cases
cards.description: Flying enemies, off-screen ranged attackers, and rooms with many small enemies are all Vekta situations. Her projectiles let you deal damage from positions where melee would be unsafe, preserving your health for the biome boss.

cards.name: Team Setup Role
cards.description: Vekta is most valuable when used as a bridge between Flux's opener and Ekku's finisher. After Flux creates the initial stagger, bring Vekta in to group targets at range and line them up before committing Ekku's area slam.

cards.name: Skill Options
cards.description: Vekta's cybernetic shurikens and force-pull mechanics make her the best option for hitting multiple targets simultaneously. Focus her Routine picks around projectile damage amplification and screen control to maximize her team support value.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Morbid Metal

questions.question: What is Morbid Metal?
questions.answer: Morbid Metal is a dark sci-fi action roguelite developed by PHO3LIX and published by Ubisoft. The game launched in Early Access on April 8, 2026. It features real-time character shapeshifting, stylish combo chains, boss fights, and replayable roguelite progression across multiple biomes.

questions.question: How many playable characters does Morbid Metal have?
questions.answer: Morbid Metal currently has three playable characters in Early Access: Flux, Ekku, and Vekta. Flux is your starting fast opener, Ekku is a heavy crowd control finisher unlocked in Biome 1, and Vekta is a ranged control specialist unlocked in Biome 2. Additional characters are planned for the EA content roadmap.

questions.question: What is the shapeshifting system in Morbid Metal?
questions.answer: Real-time shapeshifting lets you switch between Flux, Ekku, and Vekta at any moment during combat. Each character has their own cooldowns, so swapping is both a tactical and an efficiency tool. The rhythm is: deal damage, swap before your current character goes passive, and rotate back when the previous character's cooldowns have recovered.

questions.question: Where can I play Morbid Metal?
questions.answer: Morbid Metal is available on PC through Steam (App ID 1866130). The game entered Early Access on April 8, 2026. There is no console release announced at this time.

questions.question: Does Morbid Metal have a Steam Deck version?
questions.answer: Morbid Metal is playable on Steam Deck via Proton compatibility. Check the Steam page for the current Steam Deck rating and any compatibility notes for the latest Early Access build.

questions.question: What is the Morbid Metal Early Access roadmap?
questions.answer: The Morbid Metal roadmap includes additional biomes, new playable characters, expanded Corpora systems, more Routine options, and deeper hub upgrade trees. Full release timing has not been announced. Follow the official Steam news page or Discord for the latest roadmap updates.
